content.problemStatement: Chef likes trees a lot. He has a weighted rooted tree **T** rooted at node 1. There are **N** nodes in the tree numbered from 1 to **N**. Each node **v** has a value **Av** associated with it. Let's call ugliness of path between nodes **u** and **v** - C(**u**, **v**) as bitwise XOR of all values on path. Let's call ugliness of node **v** maximal ugliness among all the paths which lies in subtree **v**. For each node find ugliness of it.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains one integer T, denoting the number of the test cases. First line of each test case contains one integer N. Second line contains N space-separated integers denoting Av - the values associated with nodes. Next N-1 lines contains two space-separated integers u, v denoting the nodes connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case for each node in range from 1 to N, output its ugliness in a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
The tree is:
      1
     / \
    2   3
   / \
  4   5
The XOR values for each node are:
      1 (1)
     / \
    3 (1^2)   4 (1^3)
   / \
  7 (1^2^4)   6 (1^2^5)
The maximum XOR values for each subtree are:
      7 (1, 3, 4, 7, 6)
     / \
    7 (3, 7, 6)   4 (4)
   / \
  7 (7)   6 (6)
So the output is:
7
7
3
4
5

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a depth-first search (DFS) approach. We will calculate the XOR of all values on the path from the root to each node and store it in an array. Then, for each node, we will find the maximum XOR value among all paths in its subtree. This can be done by maintaining a set of XOR values for each subtree and updating the maximum XOR value accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * log N) - where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of nodes in the tree. The time complexity is dominated by the DFS traversal and the set operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * N) - where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of nodes in the tree. The space complexity is dominated by the storage of the tree and the set of XOR values for each subtree.
